findings.evidence: `Cylinder.lean:75` has `blockLaw_blockCylinder {μ : Measure Ω}` with no finite-measure assumption, `Cylinder.lean:133` adds the arbitrary-measure lintegral identity, and Mathlib `Integral/IntegrableOn.lean:119` uses the standard local assumption `μ s ≠ ∞` for constants on a set.
findings.fix: Replace `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` by `hfin : blockLaw μ X k (Set.univ.pi C) ≠ ∞` or equivalently `μ (blockCylinder X k C) ≠ ∞`, and use the finite-measure version as a derived corollary if needed.
findings.issue: `integral_blockIndicatorProd` requires `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`, excluding infinite measures with a finite block cylinder; the natural real-integral hypothesis is local finiteness of this rectangle/cylinder mass.

summary: The unrestricted lintegral theorem is at the right level, but the real Bochner corollary still assumes global finite measure where only finite mass of the selected cylinder is needed.
